perturbation.original_text: The Company and the Agent shall transmit all funds received from subscribers to the Escrow Agent by noon of the next business day following receipt thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company and the Agent shall transmit all funds received from subscribers to the Escrow Agent by the end of the fifth business day following receipt thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the deadline for transmitting funds to the escrow agent from the next business day to the end of the fifth business day introduces a potential conflict with SEC Rule 15c2-4, which requires prompt transmission of funds in certain offerings.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 15c2-4 requires brokers or dealers to promptly transmit or deposit customer funds received in connection with certain offerings. Delaying transmission until the fifth business day may not meet the 'promptly transmit' standard, potentially violating the rule.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.15c2-4 - Transmission or maintenance of payments received in connection with underwritings. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.15c2-4 - Transmission or maintenance of payments received in connection with underwritings. CFR prev | next § 240.15c2-4 Transmission or maintenance of payments received in connection with underwritings. It shall constitute a “fraudulent, deceptive, or manipulative act or practice” as used in section 15(c)(2) of the Act, for any broker, dealer or municipal securities dealer participating in any distribution of securities, other than a firm-commitment underwriting , to accept any part of the sale price of any security being distributed unless: (a) The money or other consideration received is promptly transmitted to the persons entitled thereto; or (b) If the distribution is being made on an “all-or-none” basis, or on any other basis which contemplates that payment is not to be made to the person on whose behalf the distribution is being made until some further event or contingency occurs, (1) the money or other consideration received is promptly deposited in a separate bank account, as agent or trustee for the persons who have the beneficial interests therein, until the appropriate event or contingency has occurred, and then the funds are promptly transmitted or returned to the persons entitled thereto, or (2) all such funds are promptly transmitted to a bank which has agreed in writing to hold all such funds in escrow for the persons who have the beneficial interests therein and to transmit or return such funds directly to the persons entitled thereto when the appropriate event or contingency has occurred. [ 41 FR 22826 , June 7, 1976] CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the deadline for transmitting funds to the Escrow Agent from the next business day to the end of the fifth business day. This contradicts SEC Rule 15c2-4, which requires prompt transmission of funds. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states that it is a "fraudulent, deceptive, or manipulative act or practice" for a broker or dealer to accept any part of the sale price of any security being distributed unless "the money or other consideration received is promptly transmitted to the persons entitled thereto." Extending the transmission deadline to five business days does not meet the requirement for prompt transmission, thereby creating a contradiction and potential violation of the rule.

perturbation.original_text: Agent shall be reimbursed for expenses as contemplated by Section 8 of this Agreement, regardless of whether the Offering is successfully completed. Any out-of-pocket expenses or commissions payable under this Agreement shall be paid in next day funds on the earlier of the initial Closing Date or a determination by the Company to terminate or abandon the Offering, and on each subsequent Closing Date thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: Agent shall be reimbursed for expenses as contemplated by Section 8 of this Agreement, regardless of whether the Offering is successfully completed. Any out-of-pocket expenses or commissions payable under this Agreement shall be paid within 60 days of the initial Closing Date or a determination by the Company to terminate or abandon the Offering, and on each subsequent Closing Date thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'next day funds' to 'within 60 days' delays payment, potentially violating wage payment laws if the agent is considered an employee in some capacity and is entitled to prompt payment.
perturbation.law_explanation: Georgia law requires employers to pay employees all wages due upon termination or resignation, or on regular paydays if still employed. A 60-day delay in expense reimbursement could violate this requirement if the agent is considered an employee and the reimbursement is deemed wages.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment terms from "next day funds" to "within 60 days." This delay in payment directly contradicts the implied requirement of prompt payment for wages, especially if the agent is considered an employee. While no specific law snippet is provided, the Law Explanation asserts that Georgia law requires prompt wage payment upon termination or resignation, or on regular paydays if still employed. The modification introduces a significant delay, potentially violating these wage payment requirements. Given the Perturbation Type is Inconsistencies - Legal Contradiction, the change creates a deadline that could violate state law regarding wage payments, aligning with the provided definition.

perturbation.original_text: In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 1,875,000 Shares on or before June 30, 2007, this Agreement shall terminate and the Company shall cause the Escrow Agent (as defined below) to refund to any persons who have subscribed for any of the Shares the full amount it received from them, without interest, as set forth in the Prospectus; and none of the parties to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other parties hereunder, except as set forth in this Section 2 and in Sections 8, 10, and 11.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 1,875,000 Shares on or before June 30, 2007, this Agreement shall terminate and the Company shall cause the Escrow Agent (as defined below) to refund to any persons who have subscribed for any of the Shares the full amount it received from them, without interest, as set forth in the Prospectus; and none of the parties to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other parties hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'except as set forth in this Section 2 and in Sections 8, 10, and 11' eliminates the agent's right to expense reimbursement even if the offering fails, potentially violating implied contract law if the agent has already incurred expenses in good faith and is not compensated for it.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_citation: Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: Even though Georgia law is specified, every contract contains implied duties of good faith and fair dealing. By removing the exception clause, the altered text allows the company to avoid reimbursing the agent's expenses incurred in good faith, which could be seen as a breach of the implied covenant, rendering the altered section legally suspect.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: good faith | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you good faith Good faith is a broad term that’s used to encompass honest dealing. Depending on the exact setting, good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties , observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent . A fiduciary relationship creates a duty of good faith between the agent and the principal . The breach of this duty of good faith can lead to liability . Failure to act in good faith is known as bad faith and is generally considered to be a level of culpability greater than negligence . [Last reviewed in January of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE business law contracts wex definitions business sectors legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The altered text removes a clause that ensures the agent is reimbursed for expenses, which could violate the implied duty of good faith. The Law Explanation states that 'the altered text allows the company to avoid reimbursing the agent's expenses incurred in good faith, which could be seen as a breach of the implied covenant.' Scraped Law Snippet 2 defines good faith as encompassing 'honest dealing' and requiring 'faithful performance of duties' and 'observance of fair dealing standards.' By removing the exception, the company could avoid reimbursing expenses, potentially failing to act in good faith and breaching this implied duty, thus creating a legal contradiction.
